name: whatsapp
context: Send WhatsApp messages using the Twilio WhatsApp Business API
info: This skill covers sending WhatsApp messages through the Twilio WhatsApp Business API, including free-form session messages, pre-approved template messages, media messages, and sandbox testing. WhatsApp messages use the same Messaging API as SMS but with the `whatsapp:` prefix on phone numbers. Template messages are required for initiating conversations outside the 24-hour session window. The Twilio Sandbox for WhatsApp enables development and testing without a registered WhatsApp Business number.

sections.title: WhatsApp setup
sections.body: Production requires a WhatsApp Business Profile registered via Twilio Console > Messaging > Senders > WhatsApp Senders. For development, use the Twilio Sandbox: send the join code from your WhatsApp to the sandbox number. The sandbox number is `+14155238886`. See [[twilio#authentication]] for credential setup.

sections.title: Sending messages
sections.body: Use `client.messages.create()` with `whatsapp:` prefixed numbers. The `to` and `from` fields must include the `whatsapp:` prefix (e.g., `whatsapp:+15551234567`). The API is identical to SMS (see [[twilio/sms#send]]) — same `client.messages.create()` method, same response structure with `messageSid` and `status`.

sections.title: Message templates
sections.body: Templates are required for business-initiated conversations (outside the 24-hour session window). Register templates in WhatsApp Business Manager, get Meta approval, then send messages matching the template pattern. Session messages (within 24 hours of user's last inbound message) can be free-form. Templates support variables for personalization.

sections.title: Media messages
sections.body: Include `mediaUrl` to send images, documents, audio, or video. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG (images), PDF (documents), MP3, OGG (audio), MP4 (video). Max 16 MB for most types. Twilio fetches the media URL and delivers it via WhatsApp. For receiving media, inbound webhooks include `MediaUrl` and `MediaContentType` fields (similar to [[twilio/sms#mms-media]]).

sections.title: Sandbox testing
sections.body: The Twilio WhatsApp Sandbox enables development without a production Business Profile. Join by texting the code word to `+14155238886`. Only pre-joined numbers can receive sandbox messages. Custom templates are not available in sandbox mode. Transition to production by registering a WhatsApp Business Profile through the Twilio Console.

scripts.name: Send WhatsApp
scripts.description: Send a WhatsApp message using the Twilio WhatsApp Business API

to.description: Destination WhatsApp number with whatsapp: prefix (e.g. whatsapp:+15551234567)

body.description: Text content of the WhatsApp message

mediaUrl.description: Array of media URLs to attach (images, documents, audio, video)

template.description: Pre-approved template name for business-initiated messages

messageSid.description: Unique identifier for the message (starts with SM)

status.description: Message status (queued, sent, delivered, read, failed)

links.context: SMS uses the same Messaging API with different number format
TwiML for replying to inbound WhatsApp messages
Account SID and Auth Token for API authentication

related.note: SMS is the fallback channel when WhatsApp is unavailable
Verify can deliver OTP codes via WhatsApp channel
TwiML messaging verbs apply to WhatsApp webhook responses
